title: DO NOT MERGE: Write output to stdout and not stderr
body:
Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

We had set the report writer to stderr so some info during
builds were being written to stderr. Move everything over
to stdout.

#### How to verify it

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:
Fixes https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2423

<!--
Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Uncomment the following comment block and include the issue
number or None on one line.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`, or `None`.
-->

<!--
Fixes #
or
None
-->

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note

```

comments.body: @rhatdan any idea why my PRs are not automatically approved when I am an approver now?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *umohnani8* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign ` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This PR concerns me. since we might be breaking scripts. @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg PTAL

@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat I am seeing a few tests failing e.g https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4739030875111424?command=integration_test#L1032. I am a bit confused on how switching to stdout is causing the exit code to be 125 for those tests, do you have any idea?

I've dabbled in the stdout/stderr stuff in Buildah in the past and it looks simple, but has complex behaviors.  I'd like to sit on this one until after we get through the Podman v3.0 delivery then take a deeper dive.

@umohnani8 the root cause of at least one failure is this:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/0ec651fd3c4a26eebaf3ca857b46138b23aec42e/tests/containers_conf.bats#L14

`buildah` should (almost) never be run in buildah gating tests. The correct usage is `run_buildah`, with the underscore.  This line is not something you touched, but by mucking with stdout/stderr the effect is:

  - old: the `$(...)` would capture stdout, which is only the container ID, which is nice and good
  + new: the `$(...)` captures stdout, including "Getting blah blah", so `$cid` is now "Getting blah blah"

This is a pretty standard existing convention: buildah and podman spit out verbose stuff to stderr, and the critical stuff (image ID, container ID) to stdout. Changing this will cause breakage worldwide.

Side note: I recommend installing [my greasemonkey log highlighter](https://github.com/edsantiago/greasemonkey/tree/master/podman-ginkgo-highlight). It is nearly impossible for a human to read podman/buildah CI logs without it.
Thank you for the tip @edsantiago, using the `--quiet` flag helped fix the test issues.

/hold

Let's find consensus.  I don't think we can merge without breaking an unknown number of users.  I am in favor of not merging as I am too afraid of the consequences.  Also, it would break my own scripts :^)

I agree with the hold. @umohnani8 my comment wasn't intended to shut-up the tests, my goal was to demonstrate the problem and why it's not fixable. The `cid=$(buildah from ...)` pattern is, I'm afraid, universal. Breaking that would affect pretty much everyone who uses buildah, and would be unpopular. I would encourage us to think very carefully here.
Beyond any doubt, I don't want this potentially merging before Podman 3.0 goes out, bumped the title for now.
Closing as we can use the `--quiet` flag to suppress extra outputs to stderr.
